name: Peña del Hierro — circular by the source of the Río Tinto
name_original: Peña del Hierro — circular por el nacimiento del Río Tinto
region_intro: Hiking around Nerva offers a unique exploration of landscapes shaped by centuries of mining activity. The region is characterized by its distinctive red-stained terrain, particularly around the Río Tinto, and features historical mining sites. These trails provide views of unusual geological formations and industrial heritage. The area primarily offers routes that traverse this unique environment, with some paths leading to points like Peña del Hierro. Best hiking trails around Nerva The most popular hiking route is Río Tinto Mining Train Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique landscape alongside the historic mining train tracks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Antiguas Minas de Riotinto - circular route, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This circular trail offers views of the ancient mining areas of Riotinto. Local hikers also love the Peña del Hierro — circular by the source of the Río Tinto, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through red-stained landscapes and past the source of the Río Tinto, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Hiking around Nerva is defined by red-stained landscapes, mining heritage, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Nerva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Nerva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Nerva?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Nerva, offering a range of options from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The area features 7 easy, 13 moderate, and 3 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes near Nerva?
A: Hikes near Nerva offer a unique landscape shaped by centuries of mining. You'll primarily encounter distinctive red-stained terrain, particularly around the Río Tinto, and historical mining sites. These trails provide views of unusual geological formations and industrial heritage, creating an almost otherworldly environment.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Nerva?
A: Yes, Nerva offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131633211/pena-del-hierro-circular-by-the-source-of-the-rio-tinto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro — circular by the source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.0 km long and takes about 1 hour 27 minutes to complete, leading through red-stained landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Nerva?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983767536/antiguas-minas-de-riotinto-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antiguas Minas de Riotinto - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.7 km path that offers views of the ancient mining areas.

Q: Are Nerva's hiking trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of Nerva's trails, especially the easier ones, are suitable for families. The unique mining landscapes and historical sites can be an engaging experience for children. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131633211/pena-del-hierro-circular-by-the-source-of-the-rio-tinto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro — circular by the source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt; for a family-friendly outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Nerva?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the outdoor trails in the Nerva area. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially near historical sites or if passing through private land. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Nerva?
A: While hiking in Nerva, you can explore several unique landmarks tied to the region's mining history and geology. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Touristic Railway for Rio Tinto Mines&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads in Nerva?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads directly outside Nerva may be limited. It is generally recommended to use a car to access the starting points of many hiking routes in the wider Riotinto mining park area. Check local bus schedules for connections to Nerva town itself.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Nerva?
A: Parking is typically available near the main attractions and starting points of popular trails, especially around the Riotinto Mining Park. Look for designated parking areas near sites like Peña del Hierro or the mining museum. Some trailheads might have informal roadside parking, but always ensure you are not obstructing access or private property.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nerva?
A: The best time to hike in Nerva is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and sometimes wet, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Nerva?
A: The trails in Nerva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique red-stained landscapes, the historical mining heritage, and the distinct geological formations that make the area a truly memorable hiking destination.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Nerva?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Nerva offers more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010392406/el-madrono-miners-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Madroño Miners' Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.7 km route that explores more extensive areas of the mining landscape, providing a longer and more strenuous adventure.
tips:
Site also known as Sierra de Padre Caro, marks the source of the Río Tinto which runs through the interior of Huelva for about 100 km until it reaches the Río Odiel.
Its name is due to the reddish color of its waters caused by the weathering of minerals with a high content of heavy metal sulfides that were found and exploited in various deposits along the riverbed.
This wonderful site and the river that is born from here is the subject of scientific studies in collaboration with NASA to analyze a possible analogy with the planet Mars.

This place seems from another planet! It is one of the most important mines (already in disuse) in the Nerva area that were exploited to extract iron ore. Today they can be visited by walking along the authorized trails to maintain safety, since these places are usually dangerous due to possible landslides.
